stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 1903, two brothers, Pedro and Manuel, set off on a horseback journey from Buenos Aires, Argentina, to New York City.
  • The 13,000-mile journey took three years.
  • The aim was to demonstrate the endurance of Argentine creole horses.
  • In 2003, Argentine veterinarian Jorge Coro retraced the route to mark the journey's centennial.
  • Coro wanted to call attention to the original ride's significance for South American horsemanship.
stem: The student wants to emphasize how far the brothers traveled. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The aim of the 1903 journey was to demonstrate the endurance of Argentine creole horses.
The brothers rode their horses from Buenos Aires to New York City—covering a total of 13,000 miles.
A century later, Jorge Coro honored the brothers by retracing their 1903 route.
The centennial of the brothers' journey was marked in 2003.

rationale: Choice B is the best answer because it states both the route and the total distance, emphasizing how far the brothers traveled. Choice A is incorrect because it explains the aim, not the distance. Choice C is incorrect because it describes the centennial reenactment rather than the original distance. Choice D is incorrect because it gives the centennial date without addressing distance.
